name: Underworld
effect: You can bring back one person or creature of your choice. The creature is restored to life exactly as they were in their prime, free from all disease and afflictions that once may have plagued them. They appear before you in any available space within 100 feet. You can do this once before you are reduced to 0 hitpoints the first time.

name: Two of hearts
effect: You gain a second heart, which activates upon your death. This heart restores 25 hit points, removes all current effects and prevents you from dying. This heart is stolen from your closest childhood friend, or--if they are dead--from the person you care for the most, the moment you draw this card.

name: Courtesan
effect: A random weapon of yours becomes intelligent, roll on an appropriate table to determine the abilities and details of the weapon's sentience.

name: Burglar
effect: You gain possession of any existing non-magic item of your choice. The item must be owned by someone and you must have seen the item at least once. The owner is supernaturally unaware of this theft for the duration of 1 week, after which they may find out on their own.

name: Merchant
effect: You may exchange any item or amount of wealth you possess for a random item of equivalent value. This exchange happens once and then the card dissipates.

name: Artisan
effect: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to a skill of your choosing (that you do not already have proficiency in), and can grant yourself advantage on one roll using that skill once per long rest.

name: Maiden
effect: You return to your youth and stay that way for 2d100 years, upon the end of which you begin aging normally, from the point of your new-found youth.

name: Savant
effect: Your highest ability score increases by 7, but all other ability scores are reduced by 3. For the next 1d3 weeks you have an irremovable level of exhaustion as you become accustomed to the shift.

name: Knight
effect: You gain the service of a 4th-level fighter who appears in a space you choose within 30 feet of you. The fighter is of the same race as you and serves you loyally until death, believing the fates have drawn him or her to you. You control this character.

name: Crown
effect: You gain an official writ of nobility and a sign of rank. This writ and sign are awarded from a random nearby society and may be anything as simple as a small guild of cheesemakers or as grandiose as the Pantheon of the Gods.

name: Reincarnation
effect: You immediately die and cannot be restored to life by any means, even that of a wish spell. You then roll on the reincarnation table for a new race with all your previous memories. Before you are born, you may reassign your ability scores fluidly, the overall number of ability points must stay the same but may be allocated as you desire. You may also do this with your class levels.

name: Throne
effect: You gain expertise in the Charisma (Persuasion) skill, meaning you add your proficiency bonus twice, instead of once, when you make ability checks with that skill. In addition, you gain rightful ownership of a small keep somewhere in the world. However, the keep is currently in the hands of monsters, which you must clear out before you can claim the keep as yours.

name: Saddlebag
effect: A griffon flies down from the clouds fully saddled and loyal to you. It can carry twice as much as a normal griffon and acts as your mount indefinitely. No other mount will ever allow you to ride it.

name: Jacks
effect: Any cards you were going to pull out of the deck return to the deck. You may choose again how many cards you wish to pick. For every card you pick, you gain 2,500gp which appear in any available containers that you own.

name: Gremlin
effect: Starting from this moment, should you consume food or water after midnight but before dawn, you lose control of your character and turn into a creature—with the monstrosity type—of the DM's choice (such as a death dog, a grick, a hook horror or a hulking whelp), wreaking havoc according to the DM's decision. When the first light of dawn touches your transformed skin, you return to your previous, normal form.

name: Troll Bridge
effect: You lose 10% from your current material wealth every time you cross anything that is or would resemble a bridge. This is any amount of trade goods, land, coins, gems, jewels, artwork, or mundane adventuring gear that you fully or partially own. Your functional gear and magic items are left unaffected. There is no prevention of this loss, except by slaying the troll depicted by this card who mysteriously gains your wealth when you lose it. The card does not dissipate nor disappear until the troll is slain, and changes as more wealth is given to the troll.

name: Golem
effect: You permanently lose one of your senses or capabilities, and cannot regain them through any normal means. Roll 1d8 and look below to see what sense you are incapable of benefitting from. This card imparts a powerful hex on the one who draws it, but it can be removed with a wish spell, remove curse or similar effect. The caster must make a spellcasting check DC 30 for the spell to be successful. 1- Blind, 2- Deaf, 3-No Smell, 4- Numb, 5-Mute, 6-No Taste, 7-lose all, can be restored by restoration spells DC 18+, 8- roll twice more.

name: Phoenix
effect: You are set aflame, completely covered in 3rd-degree burns, and draw no more cards. This effect reduces your current and maximum hit points to 0, but immediately at the start of your next turn, you stabilize and cease to be on fire. You are unable to regain hit points, except by means of raising your hit point maximum. At the end of a long rest, you regain 5 maximum hit points until you reach your hit points' previous maximum. You gain the ability to grow flaming wings from your back (wingspan 2x player height) that shed bright light for 30 ft and dim light for 60 ft around you for a maximum of 1 hour per day. Fly speed = walking speed. You now always smell of smoke.

name: Lady Luck's Scorn
effect: Not only do you suffer a specific penalty, you choose what this card does to the next unfortunate soul to draw it. This chosen effect must be a penalty of some sort. This card is not expended- but rather returns to the deck. (Current Effect: Super clumsily, you trip on everything for the rest of the day.)

name: Ettin
effect: You immediately lose a limb or body part, yet you remain alive and your hit points are unchanged. Roll 1d6 on the table below to determine what is lost. The appendage can be restored with spells and effects as normal. 1- Head, 2- Arms, 3- legs, 4- tail, 5- roll twice, 6- roll again and gain.

name: Djinn
effect: You are under the impression you have drawn the Moon Card, and are granted 1d3 wishes, which you must take on the spot. These wishes are granted in the most gruesome or backwards way. Once you make your final wish you are made aware that you drew the Djinn card.

name: Euryale
effect: While under the curse of the original gorgon, Euryale, you must take a permanent -2 penalty to all saving throws. Only one of the gods or the magic of a card can end this curse. This penalty is increased by 1 every time your proficiency bonus increases, even preemptively.

name: Frog Prince
effect: You come under the permanent effects of the true polymorph spell, which polymorphs you into a mundane animal of the DM's choice. You can still speak, but the only way to end the curse is true love's kiss, or finishing off a creature with hitpoints similar to your original form.

name: Tankard
effect: You become plagued by mimics. Over the course of time, the DM has 4d12 mimics with which they can use to replace mundane objects at any time. This happens randomly, and usually when it's most inconvenient.

name: Omen
effect: You are vexed by fate. Your next 1d4+1 rolls of major importance are treated as a natural 1. A roll of importance is at the DM's discretion, but generally should be anything that isn't an obvious attempt to circumvent this penalty.

name: Idiot
effect: Permanently reduce your Intelligence by 1d4 + 1 (to a minimum score of 1). You must draw one additional card beyond your declared draws.

name: Moon
effect: You are granted the ability to cast the wish spell 1d3 times.

name: Comet
effect: If you single-handedly defeat the next monster or group of creatures you encounter, you gain experience points enough to gain one level. Otherwise, this card has no effect.

name: Nebula
effect: Increase one of your ability scores by 2. The score can exceed 20.

name: Skeleton Key
effect: A rare or rarer magic weapon with which you are proficient appears in your hands. The DM chooses the weapon. This weapon opens an ancient door guarding something of priceless worth.

name: Lady Luck's Gift
effect: You find yourself receiving a fortuitous gift. You choose what this card does to the next lucky soul to draw it. This chosen effect must be a gift or boon of some sort. (Current Effect: DM choice.)

name: Totter
effect: You lose one major ability of your choosing. You may bestow this ability to one creature that you can see as an action, only one creature may benefit from this boon at a time. The boon lasts 1 week, and may be dispelled as an action.

name: Sun
effect: You gain a wondrous item (which the DM determines randomly) which appears in your hands.

name: Pegasus
effect: A white horse appears. While it survives, your armor class cannot drop below 13 and you gain a permanent 10ft increase in your walking speed.

name: Gemstones
effect: Nine pieces of jewelry of about 100 gp each fall out of the card.

name: The Fates
effect: Reality's fabric unravels and spins anew, allowing you to avoid or erase one event as if it never happened. You can use the card's magic as soon as you draw the card or at any other time before you are reduced to 0 hp next.

name: Forge
effect: You gain 1 legendary or artifact weapon of the DM's choosing. This weapon is unawoken and must be activated through a long and epic quest.

name: Gravestone
effect: An ancient tomb appears nearby filled with countless treasure and enemies. The tomb belongs to someone to whom you are related (and preferably close to) that previously was alive, and are immediately sent word from a courier of its existence and your right to its contents.

name: Gold ring
effect: Four halflings, each a 2nd-level Fighter, appear in front of you. They are each unique and follow your orders, believing you to be on a quest of utmost importance.

name: Pillory
effect: You are granted the ability to create two figurines of power out of live creatures. The DM will provide the magic item description for the new figurine.

name: Snake eyes
effect: Shuffle this card and any others you have drawn back into the deck. You must draw again thrice more.

name: Glass diamond
effect: You gain either a vulnerability (d20 on <10) or resistance (d20>10) to a random element. Roll 1d8 on the table below. 1-Fire, 2-Cold, 3-Lightening, 4-Acid, 5-Thunder, 6-Force, 7- Roll 1d6 twice, first is vulnerability, second is resistance, 8-Roll 1d6. If you were rolling for a resistance, it becomes an immunity. If you were rolling for a vulnerability, you become stunned until the start of your next turn every time you take this type of damage.

name: Tongue
effect: You lose the ability to speak in multiple languages. Pick one language you know, you are able to communicate brokenly with simple words in this language, you lose all other languages.

name: Revenant
effect: The next living creature you kill rises as an undead 3d6 days later and hunts you down with the intent to slay you. Once the creature has been killed, it rises again 3d6 days later (gaining a level in Fighter) and hunts you down with the intent to slay you. This cycle continues until you die or the creature is sealed away, is killed in an antimagic field, ended by deity or some other similar means.

name: Talons
effect: Every magic item you wear or carry disintegrates. Artifacts in your possession aren't destroyed but do vanish and reappear in the hands of its last resting place or owner.

name: Commune
effect: Each and every character who is near you are forced to draw a card from the deck. You suffer no consequences and are exempt from any negative effects caused by their cards.

name: The Hidden
effect: Upon picking up this card, you draw 1 more card than your declared draws. On your next draw, pick 5 cards from the deck and place them face up. The DM will inform you of each card's name, and you may then choose which one of the cards you would like.

name: Hanging man
effect: You become wanted for innumerable crimes in every major city. These crimes cannot be paid off, guards will not accept bribes and you cannot be exempt from them except by order of a King or equivalent power.

name: Ruin
effect: All forms of wealth that you carry or own, other than magic items, are lost to you. Portable property vanishes. Businesses, buildings, and land you own are lost in a way that alters reality the least. Any documentation that proves you should own something lost to this card also disappears.

name: Void
effect: Your soul is drawn from your body and contained in an object in a place of the DM's choice. One or more powerful beings guard the place. While your soul is trapped in this way, your body is incapacitated. A wish spell can't restore your soul, but divination reveals the location of the object that holds it. You draw no more cards.

name: Balance Scale
effect: Your mind suffers a wrenching alteration, causing your alignment to change. Lawful becomes chaotic, good becomes evil, and vice versa. If you are true neutral or unaligned, this card balances your stats, making them each the average of your stats. (If party member changes to evil, they may regain their goodness by singlehandedly destroying the first evil monster they encounter to become neutral, or the first party of monsters to become good.)

name: Collar
effect: A random infantile monster lays in your lap, imprinted on you as its mother.
